provider: HKU Department of Psychology BPsych Regulations and Syllabuses
note: The official BPsych Regulations and Syllabuses apply to the 2025-26 intake and thereafter. The 240-credit curriculum includes an 84-credit Psychology major, 24 credits of Social Innovation and Global Citizenship, 54 credits of University Requirements and 78 credits of another major, minors or free electives. This supplement records all 67 unique explicit course codes. The open Social Sciences prerequisite, Common Core, approved experiential or exchange routes, second AI Literacy microcredential and free electives are not fabricated as closed pools. Advanced core and elective courses have no course-specific recommended year in the official pathway and therefore remain unassigned.

supplements.ruleSummary: 84-credit Psychology major: PSYC1001 and PSYC1004; one open 6-credit Social Sciences prerequisite; five fixed advanced core courses plus one of PSYC2007 or PSYC2051; 18 advanced elective credits; and one 12-credit capstone path. The degree also requires 24 credits of Social Innovation and Global Citizenship.
supplements.evidenceGap: The Social Sciences prerequisite, Common Core, approved experiential or exchange options, second AI Literacy microcredential and 78-credit another-major, minor or free-elective component are open pools. Advanced Labs and the PSYC2007/PSYC2051 pair may serve different requirement roles, so graduation-rule allocation requires manual review and courses must not be double-counted.
